practical_and_experimental_content: Investigate and examine a local soil profile.
Investigate soil physical properties: determine soil texture, structure, porosity, and capillarity experimentally.
Demonstrate the water retention capacity of different soil types.
Test and determine soil pH.
Conduct wet tests and flame tests to identify nutrient ions in soil/fertilizers (e.g., Carbonates, Nitrates, Sulphates, Calcium, Ammonium, Phosphates, Potassium, Sodium).
Demonstrate the making of compost or farmyard manure.
Demonstrate correct methods of applying inorganic fertilizers.
Examine the external and internal structures of monocotyledonous and dicotyledonous roots, stems, and leaves.
Demonstrate the practical cultivation of a cereal crop (e.g., maize) from land preparation to harvesting.
Demonstrate the practical cultivation of a legume crop (e.g., groundnuts or beans) including inoculation and harvesting.
Demonstrate the raising of tree seedlings in a nursery.
Demonstrate soil and water conservation methods (e.g., making contour ridges, terracing, mulching, or potholing).

subtopics: 10.6.1 Anatomy and Physiology of Farm Animals
practical_and_experimental_content: Examine and identify the parts of the digestive system of a ruminant (e.g., cattle/goat) and a non-ruminant (e.g., pig/poultry).
Demonstrate physiological processes in plants: osmosis, diffusion, transpiration, and photosynthesis.
Demonstrate the practical cultivation of a root/tuber crop.
Demonstrate the raising of vegetable seedlings in a nursery bed and transplanting them.
Carry out routine management practices in a vegetable garden (watering, weeding, pruning, staking).
Demonstrate methods of vegetative propagation in fruit trees (e.g., budding, grafting, layering, and cuttings).
Identify different types of livestock feed ingredients.
Formulate a simple balanced livestock ration.
Demonstrate the practical rearing and management of a batch of poultry (broilers or layers) from day-old chicks to maturity/slaughter.
Identify common internal and external parasites of livestock.
Demonstrate methods of controlling livestock parasites (e.g., dosing, dusting, or spraying).
Identify common agricultural weeds, crop pests, and signs of crop diseases.
Demonstrate the safe handling, mixing, and application of agrochemicals.
Demonstrate the calibration of a knapsack sprayer.
Identify common pasture grasses and legumes.
Demonstrate methods of conserving forage (e.g., making hay or silage).
Demonstrate routine livestock management practices (e.g., castration, dehorning, branding/ear-notching, tail docking, and hoof trimming).
Demonstrate correct milking techniques.
Demonstrate the construction or management of a fish pond (including stocking, feeding, and harvesting).
Demonstrate the construction of a simple farm fence.
Identify suitable building materials for different farm structures.
Demonstrate simple block/brick moulding or construction of a basic farm structure (e.g., a poultry house or feed store).
Identify common agricultural hand tools and demonstrate their correct safe use.
Demonstrate the daily maintenance and safe storage of farm tools.
Identify the parts of an animal-drawn implement (e.g., mouldboard plough, cultivator, or planter) and demonstrate basic adjustments/maintenance.
Prepare simple farm records (inventory, production records, labor records).
Prepare a simple farm enterprise budget and calculate profit or loss from practical field/livestock projects.
